title: 3 rules to keeping your code healthy!
summary: Google for Developers shares three rules for maintaining healthy code, emphasizing that good identifier naming is about context rather than length. The content focuses on code quality best practices and naming conventions.
writeup: Google for Developers lays out three rules for maintaining healthy code, framed around code quality and naming conventions. The central point on identifiers is that effective naming depends on context rather than length. The takeaway: code health follows from clear, context-appropriate naming and a small set of consistent practices.
